source: Official Path of Exile patch notes and official patch-note updates

Definition.text: Fortify is represented as Fortification stacks that grant 1% less damage taken from hits per stack.

Purpose.text: Provide hit mitigation through short-duration stacking damage-reduction buff state.

Core Rules.text: Fortification gained by melee hit is stack-based, scales from damage relative to enemy ailment threshold, and stacks from melee hits and different sources; stacks are capped at a maximum amount.

Internal Rules.text: Fortification sources are validated per source and source conversion states can change how Fortify is granted (e.g., max stacks and bonus terms).

Calculation Order.text: 1) qualifying melee hit applies Fortification 2) convert hit damage vs enemy ailment threshold into stack gain 3) clamp to one gain event per 0.1 seconds 4) apply or refresh 5s base duration 5) clamp stacks to maximum.

Priority Rules.text: Fortification stack and duration behavior from the patch applies before any source-specific modifier that grants maximum stacks or converts Fortify effect into flat maximum Fortification.

Preconditions.text: A source that grants Fortify must be melee hit-based and the target interaction must generate ailment-threshold-based gain.

Postconditions.text: Each Fortification stack grants 1% less damage taken from hits and expires with Fortification duration limits.

Limitations.text: Fortification cannot be gained more often than once per 0.1 seconds per stack event; bonus mechanics can increase per-hit stacks but still obey gain-frequency constraints.

Caps.text: Default cap is 20 Fortification stacks; sources may modify maximum Fortification values via separate modifiers.

Formula.text: Damage reduction from Fortify = FortificationStacks * 1% less Damage taken from hits; stack count is clamped by stack cap and updated by per-hit gain formulas.

prevents: unbounded Fortification gain from single-hit spam

text: stack duration and maximum stack systems
Source-specific conversions that replace increased fortify effect with maximum Fortification modifiers
Recent patch revisions converted some Fortify effect sources into maximum Fortification, changing effective stacking behavior but preserving stack-based hit mitigation semantics.

Hidden Rules.text: Fortification from one melee source and different sources can stack, but all gains obey global source timing and cap constraints.

Developer Notes.text: Treat Fortify as a stack-bearing buff state with patch-versioned maximum and conversion rules to avoid collapsing older and newer semantics.

Historical Changes.text: The 3.16.0 change converted Fortify into Fortification stacks based on ailment-threshold damage and retained default 20-stack baseline with 5s duration.

text.note: Sources of Fortify grant Fortification stacks from melee-hit ailment-threshold percentage; 1% less damage taken per stack; duration 5 seconds; max 20 stacks; 0.1s gain interval.
Fortify Support no longer granted increased Fortify Duration as base duration became broadly increased.

text: A high-damage melee hit can generate multiple Fortification stacks on a single target hit.
A rare monster hit generates a larger Fortification bonus under the 3.16.0 stack model.
Fortify is not a flat 20% once-hit buff in this model; it is stack-based with duration and gain cadence.
